headlines.title: Some protests may need to stop, PM suggests, after calls for pause on pro-Palestinian marches
headlines.description: The PM tells the BBC he is concerned about the "cumulative" effect of marches on the Jewish community.

headlines.title: 'I bought a baseball cap to hide my kippah': Jews observe first Shabbat after Golders Green attack
headlines.description: British Jews tell the BBC they are agonising over whether to stick to their usual routine this Sabbath.

headlines.title: US to cut troop levels in Germany by 5,000 amid Trump spat with Merz
headlines.description: The decision to reduce the US deployment to Germany comes amid a row between the two allies over Iran.

headlines.title: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
headlines.description: The president writes that hostilities "have terminated" because of the ceasefire, arguing he does not need congressional authorisation.

headlines.title: Bomb hoax arrest after Peter Kay show evacuated
headlines.description: A teenage man remains in custody and police say no items of a suspicious nature were found.

headlines.title: These twins were born within minutes of each other - but have different dads
headlines.description: Lavinia and Michelle are the only case of twins with different fathers to be recorded in the UK.

headlines.title: Bermuda welcomes King Charles with boats, dancing and cake-cutting
headlines.description: Schoolchildren turn out to meet the monarch, with exotic birds and Bob Marley music all part of the welcome.

headlines.title: My shopping addiction hijacked my life. Now I realise what caused it
headlines.description: Children's author Sally Gardner finally found an explanation for her extravagant shopping sprees after hearing a podcast.

headlines.title: Is this the real face of Anne Boleyn?
headlines.description: A computer science team believes they have discovered a previously unknown sketch of King Henry VIII's second wife - but not everyone is convinced.

headlines.title: Revise, recall, then rest - your exam season survival guide
headlines.description: Teachers and tutors share tips for students to get through exam season.

headlines.title: There was one way we'd agreed to do Devil Wears Prada 2, says Meryl Streep
headlines.description: The sequel's stars on what's changed since the original, and why the film still resonates with women.

headlines.title: Australia wants to become the first country to eliminate a cancer - can it?
headlines.description: For the first time, Australia has recorded no new cervical cancer diganoses in women under 25.

headlines.title: Trump claims other presidents flouted war powers law. It's a mixed record
headlines.description: Both Bushes and Reagan won authorisation for wars, but Obama and Clinton ducked the requirement.

headlines.title: 'If we sleep, they bite': Rats and weasels infest camps for displaced Gazans
headlines.description: In the Gaza Strip, the daily battles are now with rats, weasels, and other pests spreading diseases.

headlines.title: The Papers:  'Burnham plans to return' and 'Four weeks from crunch'
headlines.description: Labour grandee Andy Burnham "plans to return to Westminster" and oil crunch looms lead Saturday's papers.

headlines.title: Mortgages, jobs and energy bills - how the Iran war will affect your money
headlines.description: The Bank of England's latest report reveals some interesting details on how our finances will be affected due to the Iran war.

headlines.title: Highlander stars bring Hollywood buzz to Scotland
headlines.description: Henry Cavill, Karen Gillan and Russell Crowe are in Scotland filming a remake of the cult 1980s film.

headlines.title: EastEnders star on the mental health story 'for everyone'
headlines.description: Aaron Thiara has been portraying Ravi Gulati in the soap in a story about complex PTSD.

headlines.title: Oscars says AI actors and writing cannot win awards
headlines.description: The academy that controls the Oscars on Friday issued new award eligibility requirements around the use of artificial intelligence in film.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: The Week: The Price Of The Iran War
headlines.description: Could financial uncertainty from the Iran war stop a Starmer leadership challenge?

headlines.title: LIVE at Maida Vale studios… How popular is Donald Trump in the US?
headlines.description: And why isn't there more support for Democrats?

headlines.title: Emma Stone and Ryan Gosling star in an award-winning musical
headlines.description: An aspiring actress and a dedicated jazz musician fall in love in modern-day Los Angeles.

headlines.title: All but safe? Leeds ease 'anxiety' - and increase pressure on others
headlines.description: No club has ever been relegated with 43 points at the end of a Premier League season - so have Leeds already done enough to secure survival?

headlines.title: 'Shy' Anderson on England nerves and becoming a leader
headlines.description: Nottingham Forest midfielder Elliot Anderson tells Kelly Somers about coming out of his shell to become a leader.

headlines.title: England's Wood hoping to bowl again this summer
headlines.description: England pace bowler Mark Wood says he is hoping to bowl later in the summer as he looks to come back from the knee injury that ruined his Ashes.

headlines.title: DeChambeau denies 'completely untrue' PGA talks
headlines.description: American Bryson DeChambeau says it is "completely untrue" he is looking to leave LIV Golf before the end of the year.

headlines.title: The striker quietly making a big noise at Arsenal
headlines.description: As Arsenal travel to Lyon in the Champions League semi-final second leg, BBC Sport assesses the importance of unsung striker Stina Blackstenius.

headlines.title: Norris on Miami sprint pole as McLaren return to form
headlines.description: McLaren's Lando Norris becomes the first driver to beat a Mercedes in qualifying this year with sprint pole at the Miami Grand Prix.
